title: Can You Make a Berliner Smile?
subtitle: Seven tiny social tests. One almost impossible mission: get a Berliner to soften their face.

audio.voiceDirection: Dry Berlin character voice. Short, flat, lightly amused, never shouty. English lines may carry a subtle German rhythm, but keep them clear for tourists.
audio.ambiencePromptSuffix: Subtle realistic Berlin ambience for a mobile web game, no music, no speech in the foreground, loop-friendly, low intensity.

scenes.title: You reach the counter. The line behind you is already judging your breathing.
scenes.setting: A tiny bakery at 08:17. Tray sounds, paper bags, one person sighing like it is a public service.

ambience.prompt: Small Berlin bakery counter in the morning, paper bags, espresso machine far away, quiet line murmur, one soft impatient sigh, warm but dry atmosphere

options.text: Moin. One Schrippe, please. That's all.
options.feedback: A clean order. No speech, no biography, no pastry TED Talk.

options.text: Hi! First time in Berlin, and I am so excited to try authentic local bread...
options.feedback: The smile meter felt something. It was not joy.

options.text: That one. No, the other one. Wait. The small angry one.
options.reply: All bread is angry before ten.
options.feedback: Chaotic, but locally descriptive.

scenes.title: The train is delayed. A Berliner says nothing, but somehow very loudly.
scenes.prompt: How do you share the moment?
scenes.setting: An U-Bahn platform with humming lights, a far announcement, and the spiritual weight of a cancelled connection.

ambience.prompt: Berlin U-Bahn platform ambience, fluorescent hum, distant train rumble, soft public announcement blur, footsteps on tile, calm but impatient

options.text: You make eye contact, nod once, and look back at the track.
options.feedback: In Berlin, a single nod can contain an entire friendship.

options.text: At least it gives us time to enjoy the journey!
options.reply: Please do not optimize my suffering.
options.feedback: Weaponized positivity detected.

options.text: The app says two minutes. So... seven?
options.feedback: Dry, practical, lightly doomed. Very strong.

scenes.title: A bell rings behind you. You are standing exactly where Berlin patience goes to die.
scenes.setting: A bike lane beside a busy street, wheel hiss, bell ping, the mathematical certainty that you are in the wrong place.

ambience.prompt: Berlin bike lane street ambience, bicycle passing sounds, one clear bicycle bell, traffic roll, shoes stepping aside, realistic and loop-friendly

options.text: You step out immediately and raise one apologetic hand.
options.feedback: Fast correction beats dramatic apology.

options.text: Sorry, sorry, sorry, sorry, sorry!
options.feedback: Too many syllables while still in the danger zone.

options.text: Actually, I thought this was a pedestrian-friendly shared space.
options.reply: You thought wrong in several lanes.
options.feedback: Never debate infrastructure with someone already ringing a bell.

scenes.title: The card machine is decorative. The cashier waits for your emotional growth.
scenes.setting: A late Späti with fridge hum, bottle clink, a counter mat, and the oldest cash-only truth in the city.

ambience.prompt: Berlin Späti late evening ambience, refrigerator hum, bottle clinks, small shop counter, plastic bag rustle, quiet street outside, loop-friendly

options.text: Of course. ATM nearby?
options.reply: Around the corner. Like every lesson.
options.feedback: Acceptance is the first step toward local fluency.

options.text: But it is 2026.
options.feedback: Technically correct. Socially expensive.

options.text: In my country small businesses usually accept contactless payments.
options.reply: Beautiful. Move there for this beer.
options.feedback: A comparative payment lecture rarely opens hearts.

scenes.title: You meet your neighbor while carrying glass recycling after 10 p.m.
scenes.setting: A Berlin stairwell with echo, keys, glass bottles, and a house rule laminated by someone with purpose.

ambience.prompt: Berlin apartment stairwell ambience, footsteps echo, keys, distant door, a few glass bottles clinking softly, quiet residential night

options.text: You're right. Tomorrow morning.
options.feedback: You respected the house peace. A rare diplomatic victory.

options.text: It's only two bottles.
options.reply: Then it can be only tomorrow.
options.feedback: The number of bottles was never the emotional issue.

options.text: Sorry, I'm on holiday.
options.feedback: Holiday status does not suspend physics or quiet hours.

scenes.title: A waiter says, 'No laptop on this table.' No smile. No paragraph.
scenes.setting: A small cafe with cups, low conversation, chair scrape, and a handwritten sign you somehow did not read.

ambience.prompt: Small Berlin cafe ambience, cups placed on saucers, low murmur, chair scrape, espresso machine distant, cozy but firm, no foreground speech

options.text: Fair. I'll close it.
options.feedback: You chose peace over a portable office.

options.text: Just five minutes.
options.reply: Famous last words of table campers.
options.feedback: No Berlin cafe believes in a five-minute laptop.

options.text: That's not very customer friendly.
options.feedback: The table has rights here. Apparently more than you.

scenes.title: You ask for one apple. The seller gives you the most serious apple in Europe.
scenes.setting: A weekly market stall, paper bags, crates, coins, and fruit being treated as logistics.

ambience.prompt: Berlin weekly market ambience, paper bags, fruit crates, coins, soft crowd movement, outdoor morning, no clear speech, loop-friendly

options.text: Perfect. Thanks.
options.reply: It is an apple. But yes.
options.feedback: Brief gratitude. No fruit poetry. Strong form.

options.text: Can I take a photo first?
options.reply: Of the apple? It has work.
options.feedback: The apple did not consent to becoming content.

options.text: Is it organic, local, seasonal, ethical and emotionally available?
options.feedback: You asked six questions. Berlin answered one.

scenes.title: Rain starts sideways. A Berliner lights a cigarette with the calm of a lighthouse.
scenes.setting: A tram stop in rain, traffic hiss, wet pavement, one lighter click, and everyone pretending weather is a personal choice.

ambience.prompt: Berlin tram stop in light rain, wet traffic hiss, tram bell far away, umbrella movement, one lighter click, moody but not dramatic

options.text: Good weather for staying realistic.
options.feedback: A weather complaint wearing a dry coat. Nicely done.

options.text: Berlin is so romantic in the rain!
options.feedback: Romantic optimism met waterproof reality.

options.text: Do you know where I can buy a cute umbrella?
options.feedback: Berlin accepts function. Cuteness must apply separately.

scenes.title: Nobody knows where the queue starts. Everybody knows when you do it wrong.
scenes.setting: A kiosk queue with no visible line, shoe shuffles, one newspaper rustle, and silent constitutional law.

ambience.prompt: Quiet Berlin kiosk queue, soft street ambience, shoe shuffles, newspaper rustle, tiny cough, no clear speech, dry realistic mood

options.text: Sorry, are you waiting?
options.feedback: The ancient queue spell still works.

options.text: You slide forward and hope architecture protects you.
options.feedback: The queue noticed. The queue remembers.

options.text: Is this the queue? Hello? Everyone?
options.feedback: Useful question, tragic volume.

scenes.title: You want to be friendly. This is already dangerous.
scenes.setting: A street corner with a dog, a tote bag, a coat that looks expensive in a morally neutral way, and social risk.

ambience.prompt: Berlin residential street corner, distant traffic, dog leash jingle, soft footsteps, bicycle roll, calm everyday ambience, no speech

options.text: Nice coat.
options.feedback: Two words. Low threat. Almost elegant.

options.text: You have such an authentic Berlin vibe.
options.feedback: Never tell someone they look like a city concept.

options.text: Your dog seems like it has strong opinions.
options.feedback: The dog bridge worked. Dangerous, but real.

results.title: Stone-Faced Tourist
results.tagline: The Berliner did not smile. The wall did, emotionally.
results.body: You tried. Berlin remained structurally unimpressed. This is not failure; this is onboarding.
results.shareLine: I tried to make a Berliner smile. Berlin filed a complaint.

results.title: Respectable Nod
results.tagline: A nod happened. In this city, that is basically fireworks.
results.body: You kept it short, mostly practical, and only slightly annoying. The Berliner respects your restraint.
results.shareLine: I got a Berliner nod. I am counting it as emotional intimacy.

results.title: Tiny Corner Smile
results.tagline: One side of the mouth moved. Historians are verifying.
results.body: You understood the assignment: dry, brief, useful, with one small crack of humanity.
results.shareLine: I made a Berliner almost smile. That is basically a 5-star review.

results.title: Späti-Level Charisma
results.tagline: Berlin did not hug you. Berlin allowed you to remain nearby.
results.body: You are calm under social weather, suspiciously efficient, and fluent in tiny nods.
results.shareLine: I made a Berliner smile. Very briefly. Please respect my privacy.
